project_constraints.charset_note: Gate FALHOU: a fonte do jogo não tem diacríticos — pangrama pt-BR renderiza como '@' (evidência in-game: artifacts/char1.png, char2.png). Estratégia adotada: TRANSLITERAÇÃO na gravação (acento→ASCII) no reinsert.py; a tradução canônica mantém os acentos. Ver decision_log.md.

glossary_subset.notes: Variante GRANDE do Gigiri ('o enorme'). NAO e personagem (Boro = qualificador de tamanho). Manter o composto.
Criaturas/monstros altamente venenosos (cap.12). Nome de mundo — manter.
Nome dado ao protagonista por Kuon em 11_02 (0x12668). reveal_timing: cena 11_02. Antes disso ele é sem nome.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

Erudito/escriba/mago comico e franzino do grupo do Ukon (cap.12); magia de fogo com cantico+danca longos. Fala em registro ARCAICO e comico (pleaseth/mine own; gag de cicio s->sh). Gruda no Haku como 'benfeitor'.
Criatura imortal do subsolo. Nome próprio do mundo — manter.

Guerreiro espirituoso e cativante que faz amizade com Haku ao ve-lo consertar o moinho (cap.12); lidera a 'Coorte do Ukon'. Maroro o trata por 'Mestre Ukon'. SPOILER: identidade verdadeira e reveal de arco posterior — NAO antecipar; no cap.12 e um personagem por si so. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.

lines: Características: acolhe sem pressionar ("Apenas relaxe..."); curiosa mas paciente; calor humano.
Red flags: soar fria, clínica ou impaciente; formalidade excessiva.
Haku (protagonista, narração 1ª pessoa) — `voice_criticality: high`. Predomina nas 2 cenas:
Características: sentenças curtas e definitivas; duplo sentido ("um mundo totalmente novo te espera"); ambiguidade ameaçadora.
Red flags: soar caloroso/paternal; resolver a ambiguidade (a fala deve permanecer dúbia).
Registro: erudito cômico, pomposo e arcaico; bajulador do Ukon; alívio cômico.
Características: fala arcaica/empolada (equivalente a "vós/peço-vos/meus olhos não me enganam") + gag de cicio (s→sh, ex.: "SHEEKETH"/"shir"); melodramático; gruda no Haku como "benfeitor".
Red flags: perder o arcaísmo ou o cicio (são a piada); soar moderno/neutro; explicar a comédia. Manter o contraste entre a pompa do vocabulário e a fragilidade do personagem.
Características: frases quebradas por reticências; perguntas curtas ("Quem... é você...?"); pouca pontuação forte.
Red flags: falas fluentes/articuladas demais; perder o tom de torpor; pontuação "limpa" que apaga a fragmentação.
Registro: guerreiro carismático, espirituoso, caloroso e informal; líder nato que trata Haku como um irmão mais novo.
Características: desembaraçado, brincalhão, generoso; fala direta e cativante; autoridade leve (lidera a Coorte) sem arrogância.
Red flags: soar rígido/formal/militar demais (a graça é justamente o contraste com a patente); frieza. SPOILER: não deixar a tradução insinuar a identidade verdadeira — no cap.12 ele é só "Ukon".

decisions.title: Opcode de RÓTULO DE FALANTE `53 00` + reconcile de speaker (data-driven)
decisions.summary: **Problema:** in-game, o rótulo de falante aparecia em **inglês** ("Girl") mesmo com a tradução ("Garota") aprovada e gravada. RE: o nome do falante usa um **2º opcode de ponteiro, `53 00`** (mesmo formato file-relativo do `50 00` de diálogo), que o conector ignorava. Resultado: "Girl"→"Garota"

decisions.title: Gate de charset pt-BR — método e veredito
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: likely` e exigir confirmação in-game antes de produção. **Alternativas consideradas:** - Confirmar por presença no texto-fonte — **insuficiente**: o fonte é inglês e quase não usa acentos (só `õ` e `À` aparecem em texto real).

decisions.title: Anomalia 0x33f9 — texto PT/EN corrompido na fonte
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar a linha `0x33f9` como anomalia de fonte (não traduzir em cima do lixo; tratar como linha de sistema reescrita). **Razão:** A string original já vem misturada PT/EN e truncada ("...SISTEMAS AM . RESTARTING...") no próprio jogo. Não é erro de extração. O conector deve sinali

decisions.title: Charset — transliteração na gravação (gate FALHOU)
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: false` e **transliterar** (acento → ASCII) na gravação do binário. A tradução canônica (`approved_translations.csv`, `translation_plan.json`) **mantém os acentos** (correta para QA/revisão); apenas os bytes escritos no jogo são dobrados para ASCI

decisions.title: Escopo cognitivo — 75 → 1025 linhas (cenas 11_01 + 11_02); reveal de Haku in-corpus
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Re-rodar o pipeline completo em escala (cenas 11_01 + 11_02 = 1025 linhas). Novos termos canônicos: **Kuon** (nome revelado em 0x108db), **Haku** (nome dado ao protagonista em 0x12668 — reveal agora **dentro do corpus**), **Tatari** (criatura imortal), **aperyu** (vestimenta), **

decisions.title: CORREÇÃO CRÍTICA — ponteiros são FILE-RELATIVOS, não absolutos
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Ao investigar o "opcode de início de bloco", descobri que **`50 00`+uint32 é um offset RELATIVO ao início do arquivo (Pack)**, não absoluto. Endereço da string = `file_start_do_site + uint32`. Prova: dos ~47k sites, **42.101** só apontam para string como file-relativos vs **63**

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: Well, guess the sun still rises no matter where\n
tm_voice.target: Enfim, o sol nasce em qualquer lugar, pelo visto\n

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: I am. Still... What am I supposed to do now...?
tm_voice.target: Pois é. Mesmo assim... O que é que eu faço agora...?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, you there! Could you spare a moment?
tm_voice.target: Ei, você aí! Pode me dar um momento?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, I'm sorry for bothering you, but could I ask\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: Master Ukon! It pleaseth my heart to report my\n
tm_voice.target: Mestre Ukon! É com grande satisfação que reporto que meus\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: belongings lay duly unpack'd, and await porters.
tm_voice.target: meus pertences estão desfeitos e aguardam os carregadores.

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: I am VERY tired, sir. Naught more now do I desire\n
tm_voice.target: Estou MUITO cansado, senhor. Nada mais desejo agora\n

tm_voice.speaker: Ukon
tm_voice.source: Really, Maroro? Seems like you get tired quicker\n
tm_voice.target: É sério, Maroro? Parece que você se cansa mais rápido\n

tm_voice.speaker: Homem
tm_voice.source: The way you were carrying on, you got us all\n
tm_voice.target: Do jeito que você estava, nos deixou todos\n

spoiler_guards.fact: A identidade verdadeira de Ukon (ele e mais do que um guerreiro espirituoso).
spoiler_guards.guard: Traduza as falas de/sobre Ukon como o personagem 'Ukon' por si so. NAO insinue outra identidade, patente oculta ou disfarce. Mantenha qualquer ambiguidade do original.

lines.source: Hhah--h-hah--hah, wheeze...
Seems like we were able to get away for the time\n
being, thanks to Kuon's flash grenade...
Ukon and the other survivors grind to a stop\n
around me, looking as out-of-breath as I am.
D-Doth fate preserve us...?
Hey, how long are you gonna keep clinging to me?\n
Get off, already.
Gyeh!
I disentangle myself from Maroro's vice grip around\n
my legs.
I'm exhausted. With Maroro weighing me down the\n
whole way, my body's already at its limit...
Hey, kid. You all right?
Ukon looks over at me, carrying several men on his\n
shoulders--even dragging some others with a rope.
He managed to rescue all those people despite the\n
danger...
Damn it, there's a lot of guys missing. Hope\n
they're OK.
A quick headcount reveals only about half the group\n
made it out with us.
Most of them probably just got separated, but\n
undoubtedly some wounded had to be left behind.
Man. And to think that you were on the mark this\n
whole time, anxious about a Boro-Gigiri...
Boro-Gigiri? You mean the huge one?
Yeah. My parents used to warn me when I was a kid--\n
Don't go into the woods alone or you'll get eaten.
I thought it was just a bogeyman. Never actually\n
seen one in the flesh. Didn't think they were real.
Never expected to see one pop up now, of all times...
It's just like you've been saying. Going up against\n
that thing is a fool's errand, for sure.
Sorry. I should have made myself clearer.
If I had, this wouldn't have... No. Too late for\n
that now.
Hey, no. I'm not gonna have you getting all guilty\n
over this. It's my fault for being careless.
*WHAP*
Ukon pounds his fist into his palm in frustration.\n
It seems like he's taking his anger out on himself.
So, what are we gonna do now?
Maroro. You know anything about the Boro-Gigiri?
Ho? Erst I read of the beast in some old tome or\n
other, mayhaps...
Whatever you can tell us. Anything is more useful\n
than nothing.
Hmm. Hmmmm, and hmmmm again. 'Tis a likelihood that\n
the swarm is but her brood, and she their mother.
Ere the spawning-season beginneth, one amongst the\n
gigiri doth grow as unto a titan, and giveth birth!
So engorge'd, under her bulk do her children swarm,\n
seeking as one horde what prey fate sendeth.
So it attacked us to feed its young.
Indubitably. I'faith, the breeding months are not\n
yet upon us, and the gigiri oft favor far warmer\n
climes than these...
But upon rare occasion, fate conspireth to bring\n
about such monstrosities in circumstance aberrant.
And there, I fear, lies the boundary of my\n
knowledge.
Huh. I guess that stuff about him being a scholar\n
wasn't a joke after all.
So if the Boro-Gigiri are the parents, does that\n
mean they come in pairs?
One can but assume. Alas, the fine details are but\n
esoteric, and unknown to me...
Hm.
Ukon looks lost in thought...
Something wrong?
Eh... No. I don't think so.
I'm back.
Ah, welcome back. How is it out there?
It was just as you predicted.
I knew it...
Prithee, of what dost thou speak?
Oh, they're chasing us, is all.
*Mutter*...
Sshh. They'll notice us.
Kuon tries to quiet everyone down as a murmur\n
spreads through the group...
Kuon?
Kuon's ears twitch.
*Krrkkkk*...
Barely a sound disturbs the clearing except for the\n
distant rustling of bushes.
They're heading this way...
Villagers
Gasp--!!
It'll be all right.
They're still off at quite a distance. We'll be\n
OK, I think.
At Kuon's words, everyone seems to calm a little.
Speak plain. Dost thou believe the scent of blood\n
draweth them nearer?
The scent... That's right...
This isn't good. We can't exactly erase the smell\n
so long as we have the injured with us.
Looks like we'll have to fight it.
...What?
Villager
Th-That's impossible! There's no way we'll stand a\n
chance against that monster!
A villager cries out his protest, clutching his\n
wounded arm.
I won't lie to you. It's reckless, and with our\n
forces in this shape, we can't really execute on\n
any kind of plan.
...So it really is impossible.
Even if I was right in the end, it's not exactly\n
vindicating.
In an ideal situation, I'd like to be able to fall\n
back and regroup so we can organize ourselves...
But doing that now risks luring it back to the\n
village. We have to make our stand here.
Everyone falls silent at Ukon's words.
Come to think of it, Haku?
Hm?
I'm surprised you came out of being attacked by\n
one of those things all right.
I was not "all right." It was about to eat me!
I only survived because that other Tatari thing\n
showed up and ate it before IT could eat ME.
I see. And that's why...
Ahahaha, and that's why you were about to be eaten\n
by a Tatari? You have either the worst or best luck\n
in the world, I think.
It's nothing to laugh about! I was in serious\n
trouble!
But you survived, so it made for a good story, at\n
least.
The way things are going, I'm gonna be making more\n
of those great "I almost died" stories right now.
Geez... If only a Tatari could come along and\n
swallow this one up, too...
...Huh?
What did you just say?
I'm about to make more of those "I almost died"\n
stories?
No, after that.
If only a Tatari could come along and swallow this\n
one up too?
That's it! That's it. We'll just have this one get\n
eaten by a Tatari.
Huh? Eaten up by... What? There's no way.
Haku, look around. Doesn't this place look familiar\n
to you?
At Kuon's prompting, I take a quick glance around...
Hm? This place... Could it be...?
Then it's decided. I'll go bait the Tatari and bring\n
it here.
I'll need you to... push the Boro-Gigiri off that\n
cliff over there. Somehow.
H-How can you ask--how are we supposed to actually\n
DO that?
Well... Try your best?
Try our b--You're just leaving it to us, then.
We're running out of time and this is our best\n
shot. We're lost, otherwise.
Now, hold on a second.
Ukon, PLEASE talk some sense into her.
Missy... You're really OK with this?
Eh heh heh. I may not look it, but I'm pretty good\n
at running. You won't have to worry about me.
Hey!
Ahahaha... Hey, Haku.
You know, you really are an interesting person.
With that, Kuon dashes off into the forest.
...So, what should we do, kid?
You're leaving it to me, too, huh?
Well, you and her came up with this plan, so I'm\n
assuming you can execute on it.
What am I supposed to do?
They follow the scent of blood, so... If we could\n
distract them with an even stronger scent...
Is there any of that rotten stew left?
This stuff? Yeah, there's a little.
OK. We're gonna need anyone who can still move\n
to act as decoys and peel the little ones off.
The less severely injured who can carry the\n
incapacitated will get them out of here in that\n
window.
And the rest of us will lure the Boro-Gigiri to\n
this cliff once the swarm has been baited away.
...That's about as good of a plan as I can come up\n
with right now.
...Fast thinking, in a situation like this...
Hm? Did you say something?
No, nothing. It's a good plan. It could work.
You heard him, men. Everyone OK with that?
The men around us slowly nod.
Hey, you're sure, right? I was just running my\n
mouth with whatever came to mind, so...
Considering we're improvising here, it's all good.
OK. If you're fine with it, then I've got no\n
objections.
All right. Kid! Maroro! Let's get to work!
